json.title: Manoeuvre en production laitière- ferme rhétaise inc.-nicolet
json.descriptionHtml:
Description de l’offre d’emploi

Offre d' emploi chez Ferme Rhétaise inc.-Nicolet

Description Des Tâches

Exécuter la traite; soigner et nourrir les animaux; trier, marquer et gérer les animaux; entretenir les bâtiments; aider aux activités de reproduction; au besoin préparer les animaux pour leur transport et opérer ponctuellement des machines et des équipements agricoles. Participer aux travaux des champs.

Expérience Et Exigences

1 à 6 mois d'expérience en lien avec ce travail

Capable de réaliser les tâches simples relatives à l’alimentation, à l’hygiène et au déplacement des animaux

Lieu travail principal : 1480 route Marie-Victorin, Nicolet (Québec) J3T 1T5

Lieu de travail secondaire : 2155 route Bas de la Rivière, Nicolet (Québec) J3T 1T5

Langues

  • Français parlé et écrit - Faible

json.title: Junior Data Analyst Co-op
json.descriptionHtml:
Junior Data Analyst Co-op (4 to 8-Month Term)

Location: Victoria, BC (On-site)

Start Date: May 4, 2026

Compensation: $52,000 - $58,000 (Pro-rated)

About Waterworth

Waterworth is a fast-growing, trailblazing SaaS company here in Victoria, BC. We empower more than 350 municipalities across North America to take control of their financial futures through proprietary financial modeling and analysis software.

As a mission-driven company, we value innovation, collaboration, and making a real impact in the communities we serve. Join us as we continue to grow and deliver valuable financial tools to the public sector.

The Role

We're looking for a motivated Junior Data Analyst Co-op to support the data onboarding, analysis, and forecasting needs of our Client Success team.

This is a position for a student who has completed at least one prior co-op term and is studying accounting, economics, finance, or mathematics. You'll play a key role in ensuring the accuracy and usability of client data, while helping improve processes that drive client retention and success. In addition to your data-focused responsibilities, you'll contribute to system-building efforts that support our overall operations.

Key Responsibilities:

Forecasting & Analysis:

Conduct data analysis to track client engagement, churn, and meeting outcomes, focusing on identifying patterns that drive client success.

Collaborate with the Client Success team to analyze client behavior and provide insights that help improve retention and satisfaction.

Data Onboarding & Transformation:

Import and validate client financial statements, capital project data, and utility billing information.

Transform and standardize data for seamless integration into the Waterworth platform.

Product Collaboration:

Work closely with the Implementation and Product teams to improve data handling and reporting processes.

Communicate technical findings and insights clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Documentation:

Create clear, reusable documentation for data transformation workflows and onboarding processes.

Ensure documentation is easily accessible for team members and clients.

Requirements

Educational Background

Currently pursuing a degree in Accounting, Economics, Finance, Mathematics, or a related field.

Only candidates that have already completed at least one co-op will be considered.

Please list relevant coursework in your application (e.g., managerial accounting)

Analytical & Technical Skills

Proficient in Excel: You must be able to demonstrate experience using spreadsheets to analyze data and build models. Candidates will be tested on their Excel skills in the interview process.

Excel-based projects: You will be asked to describe a previous project you have carried out that demonstrated attention to detail and technical proficiency.

Leadership & Involvement

Experience in student societies, clubs, or extracurriculars, especially where you've taken on leadership or organizational roles, is strongly preferred.

Communication & Presentation

Proven ability to explain technical concepts clearly and collaborate effectively within a team.

Experience with case competitions and research projects is an advantage.

Benefits

Downtown Office & Friendly Atmosphere

Join our you, growing team in a 100% in-person office that promotes innovation and fun. Enjoy regular socials, BBQs, office pets for morale, a ping pong table, and free snacks, creating an enjoyable work environment.

Career Development

Develop your skills with exceptional training, regular check-ins, and ongoing opportunities. Gain real-world experience in a high-performing tech SaaS company, ideal for entering the lucrative tech sector in Victoria.

Impactful Work

Our skilled team empowers communities across North America to take control of their futures, making a genuine difference in hundreds of lives.

How To Apply

Ready to make a real impact while gaining valuable experience? We want to hear from you! Please submit your resume and get in touch!

json.title: Talent Acquisition Intern
json.descriptionHtml:
Who Are We?

UniUni is a dynamic and fast-growing courier service operating in the US and Canada, dedicated to transforming e-commerce logistics. With cutting-edge technology and a relentless drive, we optimize last-mile delivery to meet the ever-evolving demands of online shoppers within an evolving industry. Handling hundreds of thousands of parcels daily, we have established ourselves as a trusted leader in the industry, ensuring speed, efficiency, and reliability for e-commerce and logistics companies across North America.

What Do We Offer?

At UniUni, we provide more than just jobs—we create pathways for growth and success. Whether through vertical promotions or lateral moves, we believe every role should expand your skills, enhance your experience, and contribute to a fulfilling career. We recognize that you're not just looking for work—you’re building a future. If you're ready to join a dynamic, fast-paced team where hard work meets an energetic and fun environment, apply today and show us how you can be part of UniUni’s success story.

Responsibilities

Full-Cycle Recruitment

  • Manage end-to-end recruitment processes for Canada Operations
  • Partner with hiring managers to understand staffing needs and job requirements
  • Post job advertisements and source candidates through various channels
  • Proactively source candidates through LinkedIn, job boards, campus partnerships, and referrals
  • Screen resumes and conduct initial candidate interviews
  • Coordinate interview processes and collect feedback
  • Support offer preparation and onboarding coordination

Talent Acquisition Operations Support

  • Maintain accurate candidate data in the ATS/HRIS system
  • Ensure hiring processes comply with provincial policies and local employment regulations
  • Support recruitment process and compliance improvement initiatives
  • Assist in reporting and tracking hiring metrics
  • Support various ad-hoc talent acquisition projects as needed.

Qualifications

  • Currently enrolled in or recently graduated from a Human Resources, Business Administration, or related program
  • Able to work full-time on-site starting from the end of March.
  • Strong organizational skills with high attention to detail
  • Excellent communication skills in English (written and verbal). Fluency in additional languages, particularly Mandarin, is an asset but not required
  • Previous intern experience in high-volume recruiting is a plus.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook); familiarity with ATS/HRIS systems is an asset.
  • Proactive, responsible, and eager to learn, with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment

What You’ll Gain

  • Hands-on experience in full-cycle recruitment, including sourcing, interviewing, and onboarding
  • Exposure to talent acquisition operations and process improvement initiatives
  • Opportunity to work closely with hiring managers and HR Business Partners, building professional HR skills
  • Potential for a full-time role after the internship based on performance and business needs

The Pay Range For This Role Is

18 - 20 CAD per hour(Toronto, Canada)

json.title: Software Developer (Intern) I - Canada
json.descriptionHtml:
Please note this posting is to advertise potential job opportunities. This exact role may not be open today but could open in the near future. When you apply, a Cisco representative may contact you directly if a relevant position opens.

Applications are accepted until further notice.

Meet the Team

Our hardworking team members are lively programming magic across the globe on teams such as Development, Information Technology, Supply Chain, Customer Experience, Security and Trust, etc.

You would play a crucial role in driving next-gen software innovations including cloud, mobile, desktop or security spaces. On any of these teams, you'll get hands-on experience working with applications that make technology accessible no matter what device they're on. See the possibilities when creativity meets develop and build solutions which will transform how we work, live, learn, and play.

Your Impact

As a software developer on our team, you will be the driving force behind crafting user-friendly, reliable software and tools that support critical technology platforms such as operating systems, networks, and databases. You will balance growing our software capabilities with hardware compatibility considerations and have the opportunity to influence develop decisions. We offer diverse opportunities across Applications Development, Database Development, Embedded Networking and OS Development, IT, Security, Test & Quality, and Web Services. You are a collaborative communicator with strong organizational skills and a passion for seeking sophisticated challenges. Driven and resourceful, you excel at managing your time efficiently, streamlining processes, and delivering high-quality results on schedule. Join us to apply your problem-solving skills and supply to innovative software solutions that make a meaningful impact.

Minimum Qualifications

  • Currently enrolled in an undergraduate degree program. Relevant fields include Computer Science, Software Development, Computer Developing, Information Technology, Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Electrical Developing, or related technical fields
  • Proficiency in at least one of the following programming or scripting languages: C/C++, Python, or Java, with evidence of recent (within the past 12 months) coursework, projects, or work experience.
  • Experience in QA, testing, and/or debugging.
  • Able to legally live and work in the country for which you're applying, without visa support or sponsorship and have an authorized co-op with your school

Preferred Qualifications

  • Knowledge of Linux, Networking concepts, OS core concepts, Embedded Development, and/or ML
  • Experience with Databases, Web Application Development, and Web Services
  • Knowledge in Computer Networking and/or Cybersecurity
  • Knowledge of Cloud Platforms: AWS, Microsoft Azure, or Google Cloud

Why Cisco?

At Cisco, we’re revolutionizing how data and infrastructure connect and protect organizations in the AI era – and beyond. We’ve been innovating fearlessly for 40 years to create solutions that power how humans and technology work together across the physical and digital worlds. These solutions provide customers with unparalleled security, visibility, and insights across the entire digital footprint.

Fueled by the depth and breadth of our technology, we experiment and create meaningful solutions. Add to that our worldwide network of doers and experts, and you’ll see that the opportunities to grow and build are limitless. We work as a team, collaborating with empathy to make really big things happen on a global scale. Because our solutions are everywhere, our impact is everywhere.

We are Cisco, and our power starts with you.

Message to applicants applying to work in the U.S. and/or Canada:

Individual pay is determined by the candidate's hiring location, market conditions, job-related skillset, experience, qualifications, education, certifications, and/or training. The full salary range for certain locations is listed below. For locations not listed below, the recruiter can share more details about compensation for the role in your location during the hiring process.

U.S. employees are offered benefits, subject to Cisco’s plan eligibility rules, which include medical, dental and vision insurance, a 401(k) plan with a Cisco matching contribution, paid parental leave, short and long-term disability coverage, and basic life insurance. Please see the Cisco careers site to discover more benefits and perks. Employees may be eligible to receive grants of Cisco restricted stock units, which vest following continued employment with Cisco for defined periods of time.

U.S. employees are eligible for paid time away as described below, subject to Cisco’s policies:

  • 10 paid holidays per full calendar year, plus 1 floating holiday for non-exempt employees
  • 1 paid day off for employee’s birthday, paid year-end holiday shutdown, and 4 paid days off for personal wellness determined by Cisco
  • Non-exempt employees** receive 16 days of paid vacation time per full calendar year, accrued at rate of 4.92 hours per pay period for full-time employees
  • Exempt employees participate in Cisco’s flexible vacation time off program, which has no defined limit on how much vacation time eligible employees may use (subject to availability and some business limitations)
  • 80 hours of sick time off provided on hire date and each January 1st thereafter, and up to 80 hours of unused sick time carried forward from one calendar year to the next
  • Additional paid time away may be requested to deal with critical or emergency issues for family members
  • Optional 10 paid days per full calendar year to volunteer

For non-sales roles, employees are also eligible to earn annual bonuses subject to Cisco’s policies.

Employees On Sales Plans Earn Performance-based Incentive Pay On Top Of Their Base Salary, Which Is Split Between Quota And Non-quota Components, Subject To The Applicable Cisco Plan. For Quota-based Incentive Pay, Cisco Typically Pays As Follows

  • .75% of incentive target for each 1% of revenue attainment up to 50% of quota;
  • 1.5% of incentive target for each 1% of attainment between 50% and 75%;
  • 1% of incentive target for each 1% of attainment between 75% and 100%; and
  • Once performance exceeds 100% attainment, incentive rates are at or above 1% for each 1% of attainment with no cap on incentive compensation.

For non-quota-based sales performance elements such as strategic sales objectives, Cisco may pay 0% up to 125% of target. Cisco sales plans do not have a minimum threshold of performance for sales incentive compensation to be paid.

The applicable full salary ranges for this position, by specific state, are listed below:

New York City Metro Area

$44,000.00 - $185,000.00

Non-Metro New York State & Washington State

$44,000.00 - $185,000.00

  • For quota-based sales roles on Cisco’s sales plan, the ranges provided in this posting include base pay and sales target incentive compensation combined.
  • Employees in Illinois, whether exempt or non-exempt, will participate in a unique time off program to meet local requirements.

Employees may be eligible to receive grants of Cisco restricted stock units, which vest following continued employment with Cisco for defined periods of time.U.S. employees are eligible for paid time away as described below, subject to Cisco’s policies:10 paid holidays per full calendar year, plus 1 floating holiday for non-exempt employees1 paid day off for employee’s birthday, paid year-end holiday shutdown, and 4 paid days off for personal wellness determined by CiscoNon-exempt employees** receive 16 days of paid vacation time per full calendar year, accrued at rate of 4.92 hours per pay period for full-time employeesExempt employees participate in Cisco’s flexible vacation time off program, which has no defined limit on how much vacation time eligible employees may use (subject to availability and some business limitations)80 hours of sick time off provided on hire date and each January 1st thereafter, and up to 80 hours of unused sick time carried forward from one calendar year to the nextAdditional paid time away may be requested to deal with critical or emergency issues for family membersOptional 10 paid days per full calendar year to volunteerFor non-sales roles, employees are also eligible to earn annual bonuses subject to Cisco’s policies.Employees On Sales Plans Earn Performance-based Incentive Pay On Top Of Their Base Salary, Which Is Split Between Quota And Non-quota Components, Subject To The Applicable Cisco Plan. For Quota-based Incentive Pay, Cisco Typically Pays As Follows.75% of incentive target for each 1% of revenue attainment up to 50% of quota;1.5% of incentive target for each 1% of attainment between 50% and 75%;1% of incentive target for each 1% of attainment between 75% and 100%; andOnce performance exceeds 100% attainment, incentive rates are at or above 1% for each 1% of attainment with no cap on incentive compensation.For non-quota-based sales performance elements such as strategic sales objectives, Cisco may pay 0% up to 125% of target. Cisco sales plans do not have a minimum threshold of performance for sales incentive compensation to be paid.The applicable full salary ranges for this position, by specific state, are listed below:New York City Metro Area$44,000.00 - $185,000.00Non-Metro New York State & Washington State$44,000.00 - $185,000.00 For quota-based sales roles on Cisco’s sales plan, the ranges provided in this posting include base pay and sales target incentive compensation combined. Employees in Illinois, whether exempt or non-exempt, will participate in a unique time off program to meet local requirements.

La Fédération de l’Union des producteurs agricoles du Centre-du-Québec est une organisation syndicale professionnelle qui œuvre activement au développement de l’agriculture dans notre région et qui s’emploie à défendre les intérêts socioéconomiques de plus de 3 000 entreprises agricoles centricoises. Elle vise l’excellence en valorisant le dynamisme, le sens des responsabilités, l’inventivité et la pérennité.

La Fédération a développé, au fil des ans, plusieurs services collectifs et individuels destinés aux entreprises agricoles de la région : aménagement, environnement, développement régional, comptabilité, gestion, fiscalité, service de paie, relève agricole, recrutement et placement de main-d’œuvre, gestion de ressources humaines sur les fermes, mise en marché, aménagement régional, prévention, formation, vie syndicale, etc. Tous ces services sont sous un même toit et plus de soixante employés y sont affectés. Nous sommes fiers de contribuer activement au dynamisme, à la diversité et à l’essor de l’agriculture centricoise.

json.title: Contracts Administrator
json.descriptionHtml:
Park Paving Ltd. has been locally-owned and operated for over 50 years and is one of the largest road construction contractors in Edmonton, and we are currently seeking a Contracts Administrator to join our team.

Whether you're a business grad looking to start your career, or someone with a civil engineering background aiming to develop a well-rounded understanding of the business, this is a great opportunity for post-secondary students or recent graduates who are looking to gain entry in the industry!

Your day-to-day responsibilities will revolve around supporting senior department staff in areas of document control, job costing, and contract billing.

Key responsibilities include:

Preparing contract documents for execution/distribution (e.g. cover letters, bonding and insurance certificates, WCB clearance letters, etc.)

  • Monitoring and tracking the status of contract and subcontract documents as part of the execution process
  • Maintaining the company's project/contract files by collecting and filing documents required for progress invoicing and subcontractor payment
  • Preparing purchase orders, progress billing documents, and change order invoices for approval

Requirements:

Must have strong proficiency with computers and common productivity software (e.g. Microsoft 365 Suite).

  • Must have an understanding of business administration, accounting fundamentals, or civil engineering technology
  • Previous contract administration experience is an asset but not required
  • Must have strong organizational and time management skills to execute a variety of tasks within tight deadlines and billing cycles
  • Must have a strong attention to detail and numeracy

Position details:

  • Wage: $25 per hour
  • Full-time, Monday through Friday
  • In-person/in-office at main company office located at 1453 - 91 Street SW, Edmonton
  • Seasonal through October 31, 2026 (with the potential for year-round work if available)

Park Paving Ltd. is one of Edmonton's largest locally owned and operated road construction contractors specializing in all aspects asphalt and concrete construction.  Our company's services include new road construction, existing road rehab (road resurfacing, and full-depth reclamation), as well as the material supply of high-quality aggregates, soil-cement, and asphalt, to a wide range of specifications.  We're constantly growing and always have room for new clients, and we welcome any inquiries for construction services or material supply.

At Park Paving our care and commitment extend beyond providing the highest quality product, and we pride ourselves on providing a stable and opportunity-rich environment to all of our employees within the construction industry.

json.title: New Graduate Private Wealth Internship Program (12-Month Internship)
json.descriptionHtml:
About Nicola Wealth:

We have a lot to be proud of at Nicola Wealth. Our sophisticated financial planning, our diversified private investment funds and their exceptional performance, our 98% client retention rate and our award-winning culture, combine to position us as a national leader in wealth management. Recognized as a Best Managed Company and one of Canada’s Top Employers, we are dedicated to upholding our firm’s values through this phase of significant growth.

By bringing together smart, dedicated and supportive colleagues from diverse backgrounds, we deliver extraordinary client service in a values-driven work environment. The collaboration, creativity and entrepreneurial spirit we were founded on provides the opportunity for you to make a real impact and build a rewarding career. We are passionate about our business, our culture and investing deeply in your growth and development to build for the future. If you have this same drive, then let’s talk!

Department Overview :

This is a fantastic opportunity to join the Private Wealth Division, a fast-growing team within the firm, responsible for providing exceptional service to our high-net worth clients. We take a sophisticated and holistic approach, providing tailored and comprehensive wealth planning and investment management solutions to satisfy each of our high-net-worth clients’ unique set of circumstances. Our approach is to help our clients build the financial, personal, and philanthropic legacies that matter to them. Our vision is to provide clients with a bespoke and personalized experience to cultivate meaningful and impactful relationships.

Purpose of the Role::

Do you have a passion for delivering exemplary client service along with a keen attention to detail and a desire to grow in your career?

As a member of the Internship Program, you will be part of our high-performing Associates Team, learning from the ground up while working alongside Associates and our best-in-class Wealth Advisors. We focus on personal and professional growth and are committed to helping you maximize your potential to achieve success and to inspire career paths such as people leadership, Wealth Advisors, and career Associates.

This is a one-year internship program, beginning in June 2026. Upon successful completion of the program, you will have a chance to be hired as a full-time Wealth Planning Associate. Please see our brochure to learn more about the program.

As part of our hybrid working arrangement, you will be expected to be in the office full-time for the first four weeks of your employment, followed by a minimum of three days in the office per week for the remainder of your internship program.

We welcome applications from candidates currently based in our hub cities: Toronto, Calgary, Kelowna, Vancouver, and Victoria.

Qualifications and Key Attributes:

  • Completion of post-secondary education in finance or a related field.
  • Desire to enroll in and complete the Canadian Securities Course (CSC) during the length of the program. 
  • 1-2+ years of professional experience. We also encourage applications from individuals interested in transitioning into a career in wealth management.
  • Interest in pursuing a career in wealth management. 
  • Exemplary organizational skills with a keen eye for detail. 
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 
  • An analytical mindset. 
  • Resourceful and a self-starter. 
  • Exceptional interpersonal and relationship management skills. 
  • Ability to collaborate and work well as part of the wider team. 
  • Curious, inquisitive and the ability to adapt to change. 

Compensation & Benefits Package:

At Nicola Wealth, our people grow our business, delight our clients, and live our values every day. Our comprehensive compensation and benefits package reflects our appreciation for this commitment.

This position comes with a competitive base salary hiring range of $50,000-$60,000, dependent on experience, skills, qualifications, and internal equity.

As part of our total rewards package, we also offer:

  • Performance-based bonus.
  • Paid time off for vacation, studying, and volunteerism, along with flexible personal leave days because balance is important.
  • Extended health, dental and mental health coverage
  • Professional development programs, coaching and support.
  • An award winning, inclusive, and supportive culture.

Application closing date is April 10, 2026.

This role is a current vacancy within Nicola Wealth.

Please include both a cover letter and resume in support of your application. We are an inclusive, equal opportunity employer. For more information about this and other roles: www.nicolawealth.com/careers.

It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. Should you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process please contact us at accessibility@nicolawealth.com and we will work with you to meet your needs. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Nicola Wealth policies.

Thank you for your interest in a career with Nicola Wealth!

json.companySlogan: Wealth management beyond stocks and bonds.
json.companyDescription:
Founded in 1994, Nicola Wealth Management Ltd (Nicola Wealth) has over 30 years of investment management experience and has navigated through numerous market cycles, economic downturns, and geopolitical events. Over that time, we have built a team of investment professionals, managing a broad range of investment solutions which span traditional asset classes, such as Canadian and global equities and fixed income, as well as private asset classes including hard asset real estate, infrastructure, private debt, mortgages, and private equity.

Today, Nicola Wealth is one of the largest independent (non-bank) private investment counselling firms in Canada, with approximately $16.8 billion of assets under management as of Jan 1, 2025.

For more information, please visit www.nicolawealth.com.

Nicola Wealth Management Ltd. (Nicola Wealth) is registered as a Portfolio Manager, Exempt Market Dealer and Investment Fund Manager with the required securities commissions.

json.title: Probationary Kindergarten Teacher
json.descriptionHtml:
The Golden Hills School Division invites applications for a part-time (0.5 FTE) probationary teacher at Prairie Christian Academy in Three Hills, AB for the 2026/2027 school year. The teaching assignment is for: Kindergarten.

Prairie Christian Academy (K – 12) is an alternative Christian school with 330 students. Applications are sought from teachers who have demonstrated excellence in their teaching and who have the ability to carry out their job duties according to the vision, mission, and values of Prairie Christian Academy and in a manner consistent with the PCA Statement of Faith. Please include a Pastoral reference along with references that demonstrate your suitability for this position.

The successful candidate will demonstrate strong 21st century teaching skills including learning design that ensures high student engagement, critical thinking skills, inquiry, and the use of technology as a learning tool.

Qualified candidates must apply to our district electronically using the above APPLY button. The competition will remain open until the position is filled. Applications received prior to 8:00 AM on Tuesday, March 17, 2026, are assured careful consideration.

Only those applicants short-listed will be contacted. We thank all applicants who have applied for their interest in the Golden Hills School Division.

Human Resources Department

Golden Hills School Division

435 A Highway # 1

Strathmore, Alberta T1P 1J4

Phone: 403-934-5121

For assistance with the online application, please contact ApplyToEducation at 1-877-900-5627 or email .
